title: S3 consistency, storage classes, and lifecycle economics
description: Explains strong read-after-write consistency, storage-class tradeoffs (Standard/IA/Glacier), retrieval latency, and lifecycle transition costs.
body: Use when choosing S3 storage or debugging retrieval cost/latency. Do not put hot, frequently-read objects in Glacier or assume IA is free to read. Actions: rely on S3 strong read-after-write consistency for new and overwritten objects, match access pattern to class (Standard for hot, Standard-IA for warm with a per-GB retrieval fee, Glacier/Deep Archive for cold with restore latency), and use lifecycle rules mindful of the minimum-storage-duration charge and per-object transition cost. Validation signals include retrieval latency matching the class and lifecycle metrics showing expected transitions. Risk boundary: a lifecycle rule that deletes or deep-archives production data is human-gated.
